What we believe it means: Monthly — every month
Example from notes: Jan - M - 1pts - OT

What we believe it means: Quarterly — every 3 months
Example from notes: Jun - Q - 2pts - OT, DS - 2 x LP, 2 x Micro

What we believe it means: Half-yearly — every 6 months
Example from notes: Mar - H - 2pts - OT, TI, DS - 3 x LP, 3 x Micro, CWST

What we believe it means: Annual — once per year
Example from notes: Jan - A - 6pts - PT, TI, DS - 4 x LP

What we believe it means: Weekly — every week
Example from notes: Weekly flushing of low used outlets - W - 1pt

What we believe it means: Bi-monthly — every 2 months
Example from notes: Feb - BiM - 2pts - OT, CT

What we believe it means: Outlet Temperature — temperature monitoring at each registered hot/cold water outlet. One inspection per outlet job.
Example from notes: Apr - M - 1pts - OT / Sep - H - 2pts - OT, TI, DS

What we believe it means: Tank Inspection — cold water storage tank inspection. Includes visual check of tank, lid, insulation, water quality. Always 6-monthly (H).
Example from notes: Mar - H - 2pts - OT, TI, DS - 3 x LP, 3 x Micro, CWST

What we believe it means: Cold Tank Inspection — older synonym for TI. Identical meaning. Confirmed in legacy visits.
Example from notes: H - 4pts - CTI, DS

What we believe it means: CWST — Clean and Spray disinfection. ONLY when CWST appears as a token in the header (comma-separated list). If CWST appears after 'from' in a DS description, it's a location, not a service.
Hydrocert Job Type: Cold water storage tank - Clean and Spray disinfection form
Example from notes: Mar - H - 2pts - OT, TI, CWST, DS - 3 x LP → CWST in header = Clean & Spray DS - 2 x Micro from CWST → CWST in description = location

What we believe it means: Domestic Sample — umbrella token for water sampling. Total inspections = sum of all LP + Micro + DOMESTIC + POTABLE counts that follow.
Example from notes: DS - 3 x LP, 3 x Micro = 6 inspections DS - 4 x DOMESTIC Micro, 4 x LP = 8 inspections

What we believe it means: Legionella Pneumophila Sample — count N in 'N x LP' = number of Domestic Sample inspections. Location qualifiers after 'from' are descriptions only — they DO NOT change the count.
Example from notes: DS - 3 x LP from CWSTs = 3 Domestic Sample inspections 7 x LP from linked CWSTs, 1 x Irrigation Tank, 3 x HWS = 7 LP total

What we believe it means: Post Flush Legionella Analysis — variant of LP for post-flush/disinfection validation. Used after a system has been flushed and disinfected.
Example from notes: V75506 — POST FLUSH visit

What we believe it means: Microbiological Sample — domestic context = Domestic Sample. Cooling context (Suite=COOLING, Cooling Tower, CTM/CTQ/CTW) = Cooling System inspection.
Hydrocert Job Type: Domestic Sample (domestic) / Cooling System on-site chem testing (cooling)
Example from notes: DS - 3 x Micro = 3 Domestic Samples 2 X Micro (Suite = COOLING) = 2 Cooling System inspections

What we believe it means: Greywater Sample — sample from greywater/recycled water (e.g. irrigation). Domestic Sample with 'Suite = GREY' context.
Example from notes: Greywater Sampling – 6 x LP and 6 x Micro (Suite = GREY) = 12 Domestic Samples

What we believe it means: Domestic (sample sub-type) — qualifier (e.g. '4 x DOMESTIC Micro'). Counts toward DS total like LP or Micro.
Example from notes: 4 x POTABLE Micro, 4 x DOMESTIC Micro = 8 Domestic Sample inspections

What we believe it means: Potable water sample — counts toward DS total
Example from notes: 4 x POTABLE Micro

What we believe it means: Microbiological — Healthcare (more stringent)
Example from notes: V73945 — healthcare site

What we believe it means: Giardia parasite test — specialty
Example from notes: Hamilton-style sites

What we believe it means: Cryptosporidium sample — specialty parasite, hospital sites
Example from notes: Hospital sites

What we believe it means: Ralstonia bacteria test — healthcare sites
Example from notes: V73945 (HC sites)

What we believe it means: Greywater spray system microbiological
Example from notes: V23698 — GREYWATER SPRAY

What we believe it means: Pseudomonas spp. test — counts toward Micro total
Example from notes: 4 x Micro (Suite = HYDROCERT-PSEUD-SPP)

What we believe it means: Legacy synonyms for DS (used in 2019-2020 visits only). Parse the LP/Micro sub-counts that follow.
Example from notes: Feb - H - 4pts - TI, OT and DM - 4 x Lp and 4 x Micros Feb - H - TI, OT, S - 2 x Micro 2 x LP

What we believe it means: Showerhead Descaling — clean, descale, disinfect showerheads. SD = older synonym, treat identically.
Example from notes: Jun - H - 2pts - OT, SH, DS - 3 X LP, 1 x Micro Feb-H-3pts-TI, SD, DS

What we believe it means: Calorifier / Water Heater Temperature — monthly temp check on hot water cylinder. NOT cooling tower (see CTM/CTQ/CTW).
Example from notes: Jan - M - 2pts - OT, CT / Jan - H - 2pts - OT, CT, CF, TI

What we believe it means: Calorifier / Water Heater Drain Flush — physical drain to remove sediment. Half-yearly or quarterly.
Example from notes: Jan - H - 2pts - OT, CT, CF, TI

What we believe it means: Thermostatic Mixing Valve Servicing — annual service and failsafe test.
Example from notes: Annual TMV Service - over 4 days - April | 51 TMVs listed

What we believe it means: Water Feature OR Little-Used Outlet Flushing. Determined by context: with chem/pH/conductivity/balance/WFmicro = Water Feature on-site testing. Standalone with no chem context = flushing of little-used outlets.
Hydrocert Job Type: Water feature - On site chem testing form / Little used outlet flushing
Example from notes: WF - on-site chem analysis - 1 x WFmicro = Water Feature Weekly flushing - WF standalone = Little Used Flushing

What we believe it means: Water Feature Monthly / Quarterly — on-site chemical testing. Often paired with LP/Micro from water feature (Suite = WFMPSEUD).
Hydrocert Job Type: Water feature - On site chem testing form
Example from notes: JAN-M-6pts-CTM – 2xCOOLING TVC, WFM- 1xWFMONTHLY

What we believe it means: Hydrogen Peroxide chemical testing — monitoring tank H2O2 levels (used in continuous disinfection systems). NOT a sample type — an on-site reading test. Found ~14K times across 115K visits.
Hydrocert Job Type: H2O2 / Hydrogen Peroxide on-site testing (custom job type)
Example from notes: Jan - M - 2-pts - H202 testing from Main dosing pump, mezz gents WHB, 5th Floor Monthly H202 testing, Monitoring tank, record PPM Level

What we believe it means: Chlorine Dioxide on-site testing — PPM levels at tanks (target 0.1-0.5ppm) and outlets. On-site reading only. Found ~3K times.
Hydrocert Job Type: ClO2 / Chlorine Dioxide on-site testing (custom job type)
Example from notes: Monthly - CL02 testing - 2 x mains tanks, 1 x basement outlet Weekly CLO2 testing

What we believe it means: Legionella Risk Assessment — full site risk audit. Distinct from sampling/inspection visits. Annual or biennial.
Example from notes: LRA of current management systems LRA full

What we believe it means: Closed System sample — sample from a closed heating/chilled water circuit. Three sub-types by context: default = main circ, 'Remote' keyword = Remote sample, 'on-site chem' keyword = On-Site testing.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ / Remote / On-Site
Example from notes: CS - 1 x Chem Basic and 1 x BSRIA Bacti from LTHW CS - 4 X Remote Samples – 8 BSRIA Bacti and 8 Chem Remote

What we believe it means: Fan Coil Unit — remote sampling point inside the closed system. Each FCU = 1 Remote inspection. 'FCU x N' means N units = N Remote inspections.
Example from notes: 4 X Remote Samples – 8 BSRIA Bacti and 8 Chem Remote Basic FCU x4 from LTHW loop

What we believe it means: BSRIA microbiological analysis — UK BSRIA standard bacteriological test. Sample type: Bsria Bacti. Variants: BRSIA, BSRAI, BRSRA = same thing (typos in older notes).
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Bsria Bacti)
Example from notes: CS - 4 x BSRIA BACTI from LTHW

What we believe it means: Closed System Chemical Basic — standard chemistry analysis on closed system. Sample type: Chem Basic. Product CAR-CS-BASIC.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Chem Basic)
Example from notes: CS - 4 x CHEM BASIC from CHW

What we believe it means: Chem Basic with Ammonia testing — variant including ammonia parameter. Product CAR-CS-BASICNH3.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Chem Basic + NH3)
Example from notes: V142234 — CHEM BASIC+NH3

What we believe it means: Closed System Remote Full lab analysis — full chemistry remote sample (independent lab). Distinct from Remote Basic. Product CAR-CS-RFULL-LAB.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - Remote sample (Chem Remote Full)
Example from notes: V14723 — full chem remote

What we believe it means: Closed System Post Dosing analysis — validation sample after chemical dosing. Product CAR-CS-POST-DOSE.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (post dosing)
Example from notes: Following dosing visits

What we believe it means: BSRIA Validation suite — for new or freshly cleaned systems. Sample type: Bsria Validation. Product CSM-BSRIAVAL.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Bsria Validation)
Example from notes: New build commissioning

What we believe it means: BSRIA Bacti combined with Sulphate-Reducing Bacteria analysis. Product CSM-B&SRB.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Bsria + SRB)
Example from notes: V142234 — CLOSED BASIC & SRB

What we believe it means: Closed System On-Site chem testing — engineer tests on-site (no lab). Distinct job type from main circ / remote. Product CAR-CS-OSB.
Example from notes: CS on-site chem - 4 x BSRIA BACTI

What we believe it means: Chem Basic with Glycol parameter — for closed loops with glycol antifreeze (e.g. ASHP, RAC, condenser systems). Variant of Chem Basic.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Chem Basic + Glycol)
Example from notes: CS - 1 X CHEM BASIC+GLYCOL and 1 X BSRIA BACTI ASHP LTHW CS - 3 x on-site chem (PLUS ONSITE GLYCOL TEST)

What we believe it means: Chem Basic with Ammonium / Ammonia parameter — similar to CHEM BASIC + NH3 but specifically NH4 ammonium ion testing.
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Chem Basic + NH4)
Example from notes: CS (2 x BSRIA VALIDATION and 2 x BASIC + AMMONIUM) CS - 3 x BSRIA BACTI, 3 x CHEM BASIC & NH4

What we believe it means: Chem Validation sample — full chemistry validation suite (counterpart to BSRIA Validation, often as RAPID variant).
Hydrocert Job Type: Closed system - main circ sample (Chem Validation)
Example from notes: 1 x CHEM VALIDATION+AZOLES (select RAPID - Chem Validation in Latis app)

Token: CS Dosing (/ DOSING / PRE DOSE / POST DOSE)
What we believe it means: Closed System Chemical Dosing — separate visit/inspection type for adding chemical inhibitor to closed loops. Includes pre-dose sample + dose + post-dose validation. Found ~1K times.
Example from notes: LTHW DOSING - Closed System Chemical Dosing pre PTSA Dosing Sample VISIT 2 -dosing - 5 litres WTP C51

What we believe it means: Re-sampling visit — follows up a failed/raised sample with another collection from same locations. Not a base inspection — triggered by failed result.
Hydrocert Job Type: Same as the original inspection (DS / CS / Pool, etc.)
Example from notes: To resample the GF 6TH FORM BOYS WHB HWS RESAMPLE following CWST C&D

What we believe it means: Total Viable Count from cooling tower water. Sample type: Cooling TVC. Product COOL.
Example from notes: 10 X TVC (SUITE=COOLING) from Towers = 10 Cooling System inspections

What we believe it means: Total Viable Count from cooling supply/feed point. DIFFERENT job type from tower TVC.
Hydrocert Job Type: Cooling System Feed on-site chem testing
Example from notes: 1 x TVC from Supply = Cooling System Feed inspection "feed TVC" or "supply TVC" in notes

What we believe it means: Cooling Tower Weekly — on-site chemistry monitoring. Product CAR-EC-W. NOT calorifier (CT) — different system.
Example from notes: V23350 — CTW visit

What we believe it means: Cooling Tower Monthly — on-site chemistry monitoring. Product CAR-EC-M.
Example from notes: JAN-M-4pts-CTM – 2xCOOLING TVC

What we believe it means: Cooling Tower Quarterly — often Feed sample. Different job type from CTM/CTW. Product CAR-EC-Q.
Hydrocert Job Type: Cooling System Feed on-site chem testing
Example from notes: Jan - CTW - weeks 1, 2, CTQ (incl. cooling LP and feed samples)

What we believe it means: Cooling TVC + Pseudomonas combined test. Product COOLINGPSEUD.
Example from notes: High-risk cooling sites

What we believe it means: Cooling extra lab chemistry — suspended solids etc. Product CAR-EC-CTEXTRA.
Example from notes: V144782 — Suite=CTEXTRA

What we believe it means: Pool Microbiological Sample — monthly micro from swimming pool/spa. Lighter version (micro test only).
Example from notes: Feb - M - 1pts - OT, CT, Pool Micro Jan - M - 1pt - from Swimming Pool (Suite = POOL)

What we believe it means: Pool & Spa On-Site Chemical Analysis — full chemistry inc. pH, chlorine, TDS, temperature. P&SM = monthly, SQ = spa quarterly.
Example from notes: Jan - H - 2pts - OT, CT, CF, TI, On site Pool Chem and Micro Jan - M - 2pts P&SM - Pool & Spa Disinfection Chem On site (pH, TDS, Temp...)

What we believe it means: Pool water analysis sample — when '1 x Micro (Suite=POOL)' appears, this is a pool sample, NOT a Domestic Sample. Product POOL.
Example from notes: V149141 — 1 x Micro (Suite = POOL)

What we believe it means: Spa water analysis sample — equivalent of Suite=POOL but for spa. Product SPA.
Example from notes: Spa-equipped sites

What we believe it means: LP filtered method on pool/spa water. Sample type: Legionella. Product LA-Filter.
Example from notes: V149141 — 1 x LP (Suite = LPFILTERED)

What we believe it means: RO/Steam Boiler chemistry analysis — reverse osmosis water + steam boiler chem. Product CAR-RO CHECK.
Example from notes: V14684 — CAR - RO, FEED, 3 x STEAM BOILERS

What we believe it means: Steam Boiler suite — multiple sub-products: Make-Up, Feed, Condense, Raw water.
Example from notes: V14684 — multiple steam boiler samples

What we believe it means: Hot Water System — outlets on the hot water distribution
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Count toward LP/Micro total in DS block

What we believe it means: Cold Water System — outlets on the cold water distribution
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Count toward LP/Micro total in DS block

What we believe it means: Bib Tap — external tap used as sampling point
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Count toward LP/Micro total in DS block

What we believe it means: Rainwater Harvesting — collection system used for irrigation/grey flushing
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Count toward DS total; may also generate TI if 'RWH Tank Inspection' specified

What we believe it means: Rainwater Harvesting Tank — physical tank
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location within DS or TI inspection

What we believe it means: Above Ground Storage Tank — used as OT or sampling point
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): OT location qualifier or DS sampling point

What we believe it means: Boosted Cold Water System — pressurised cold water with pump
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): TI location qualifier; DS sampling point

What we believe it means: Domestic Water Tank — cold or hot water storage tank
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): DS or TI location qualifier

What we believe it means: Temperature-Controlled System — group of sentinel outlets
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): DS location group — count toward LP/Micro total
Comment: can also be towards OT - temperature monitoring

What we believe it means: Domestic Hot Water System — system identifier
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): CS or DS context qualifier — not a separate job

What we believe it means: Legionella Pneumophila Samples — plural of LP
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Same as LP — count each as 1 Domestic Sample

What we believe it means: Wash Hand Basin — outlet location label on OT job
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Not a service token — OT location label only

What we believe it means: Closed Water System — Remote Sample location label (e.g. CSRS 1, CSRS 2). Identifies which remote sampling point. Found 683 times as itemLocation.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location label on Closed System Remote inspection — not a service token.

What we believe it means: Point of Use — under-sink water heater or local water supply unit. Outlet location label.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location qualifier on OT or DS inspection.

What we believe it means: Calorifier Drain — sampling point on a calorifier drain. Often used for LP/Micro samples.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location qualifier on DS or CF inspection.

What we believe it means: Condenser/Condensate — main circulation point on a condenser water system. Used as itemLocation.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location label on Closed System inspection (condenser circuit).

What we believe it means: Ground Source Heat Pump — system identifier for closed loop heat pump systems.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location/system context label on CS inspection.

What we believe it means: Air Source Heat Pump — system identifier (already used in CS Glycol context).
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location/system context label on CS inspection.

What we believe it means: Refrigerant cooling / Roof AC system — identifier for roof-level AC closed loops with glycol.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location/system context label on CS inspection.

What we believe it means: Process Cooling Water — industrial process cooling system identifier.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location/system context label on CS inspection.

What we believe it means: Plate Heat Exchanger — closed system component identifier.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location/system context label on CS inspection.

What we believe it means: Sentinel outlet — designated outlet for monitoring (furthest/least-used). NOT a service.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location label on DS inspection.

What we believe it means: FCU pattern (Fan Coil Unit count). Each unit = 1 Remote inspection. Already covered by FCU token.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): See FCU above.

What we believe it means: Low Pressure Hot Water (LPHW) or Secondary LTHW — system variants of LTHW. Used as system identifier on Closed System inspection.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location/system context label on CS inspection.

What we believe it means: Heat Interface Unit — in-apartment closed loop unit (variant of FCU). Each HIU = 1 Remote sample.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Closed system - Remote sample (per HIU unit).

What we believe it means: Water Source Heat Pump — closed loop heat pump system identifier (variant of GSHP / ASHP).
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location/system context label on CS inspection.

What we believe it means: Softened Water Storage Tank — CWST variant for softened water (irrigation, cooling, processed).
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location qualifier on TI / DS inspections.

What we believe it means: Mains Water Supply — sampling location at incoming mains water.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Location qualifier on DS inspection.

What we believe it means: Oxidation-Reduction Potential — chemistry measurement parameter (millivolts). Reported alongside pH, TDS for cooling and pool water.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Parameter on Cooling chem / Pool chem inspection (not separate job).

What we believe it means: Building Services Research & Information Association — UK testing standard. 'BSRIA Bacti' = bacteriological test. Multiple typo variants all mean the same.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier within CS or DS. Ignore as a job-type token.

What we believe it means: Azole corrosion inhibitor — chemical additive in closed systems (LTHW/CHW). 'Azole testing included' means CS sample also tested for azole levels.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on CS inspection. Does NOT generate additional job.

What we believe it means: Combined Heat & Power — closed system loop type. CHP is a sub-variant of CS.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): CS system sub-type. Treat as CS main circ.

What we believe it means: Langelier Saturation Index — pool/spa chemistry calculation for scale/corrosion balance.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Pool Chem inspection. Does NOT generate additional job.

What we believe it means: Out of Hours — visit must be done outside normal business hours.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Scheduling annotation only. Not an inspection.

What we believe it means: Pattern in DS — identifies a Domestic Sample as Potable testing suite. Used as analysis qualifier on Domestic Sample inspection.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Domestic Sample. Sample type = Potable/Domestic.

What we believe it means: Pattern in DS — identifies a Domestic Sample as Greywater. Same effect as GREY token.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Domestic Sample. Sample type = Greywater.

What we believe it means: Pattern on Water Feature monthly micro samples.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Water Feature inspection.

What we believe it means: Pattern in CS — identifies the BSRIA Bacti analysis suite. Same effect as BSRIA BACTI token.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Closed System inspection. Sample type = Bsria Bacti.

What we believe it means: Pattern in CS — specifies the chem analysis variant: BASIC, VALIDATION, BASIC+SRB, BASIC+NH4, BASIC+GLYCOL.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Closed System Chem inspection — the specific value after = determines the product.

What we believe it means: Pattern on Cooling TVC samples that include Pseudomonas test.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Cooling System inspection. Sample = Cooling TVC + Pseud.

What we believe it means: Pattern on LP samples that must be taken in 1L filter bottle — same as Suite=LPFILTERED.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Pool/Spa LP inspection. Sample = Legionella (filtered).

What we believe it means: Same as Water Feature Monthly product when used as a sample suite tag.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Water feature on-site testing.

What we believe it means: Explicit form of BSRIA Bacti suite (=BSRIA BACTI). Same effect as BSRIA token.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Closed System inspection. Sample = Bsria Bacti.

What we believe it means: BSRIA Validation suite. Same effect as BSRIA Validation token.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Closed System inspection. Sample = Bsria Validation.

Token: Suite=CHEM BASIC (/ VALIDATION / POST DOSE / REMOTE FULL RAPID / etc.)
What we believe it means: Variants of CHEM suite: BASIC / VALIDATION / VALIDATION RAPID / POST DOSE / REMOTE FULL / REMOTE FULL RAPID.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Closed System Chem — the specific variant determines the product code.

What we believe it means: Generic Legionella suite tag — alternative to Suite=LPFILTERED.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Domestic Sample (Legionella).

What we believe it means: Combined Potable + Domestic suite tag.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Domestic Sample (Potable/Domestic).

What we believe it means: Greywater Spray system suite. Same effect as GREY SPRAY token.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Domestic Sample (Greywater Spray).

What we believe it means: Water Feature Monthly + Pseudomonas combined test.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Qualifier on Water Feature inspection.

What we believe it means: Water Feature Weekly — new frequency variant for water features.
How to handle (Location/Qualifier only): Water feature on-site testing (weekly cadence).
